extraction.one_line_summary: BAUHAUS Phil won the Tirreno-Adriatico stage 7 sprint by switching to the opposite side of the road after finding no space on the sunny side approaching the flamme rouge.
tactical_patterns: Israel Premier Tech controlled lead-out through technical final 3km sequence but NIZZOLO failed to convert their positional dominance.
BAUHAUS switched road sides late when blocked, exploiting teams fixating on sunny-side positioning through curved technical approach.
Bahrain Victorious sprinter survived without dedicated lead-out where Intermarché's KRISTOFF failed despite team numbers after morning crash.
Technical pinch at flamme rouge after right-hander sub-3km eliminated pure speed sprinters, favoring positioning over power.

rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: negative: Had perfect Israel Premier Tech lead-out through technical finale but failed to finish top-3
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: Team disrupted by morning CORT crash yet positioned well but booked/tangled in approach sprint
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: positive: Showed tactical awareness switching road sides when boxed, converted poor position to win without lead-out train
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: neutral: Dominated GC/points/youth classifications throughout week, completing clean sweep of non-mountains jerseys
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: Tangled with FIORELLI approaching finale, unable to contest despite surviving technical circuits
